title: Lead Workspace foundation (canonical composed operator view)
originalPrompt: Build a bounded Lead Workspace foundation, not WS4 or another Deal Card migration. Preserve accepted WS1 canonical acreage/spatial basis, WS2 research completeness/evidence language, and WS3 unified readiness/approved-strategy status. Compose existing lead, property, seller, communication, property intelligence, evidence, market, comparables, valuation, strategy, acquisition, task/blocker/decision, agent work, and Activity records through one versioned read model and stable department-output contract. Use adapters; do not duplicate calculations or replace the backend/database. Provide a responsive live Acquisitions workflow for desktop and Galaxy S24 Ultra widths, using real records and honest unavailable/unknown states. Quarantine the new flow from legacy Deal Card tab aggregation without deletion. Test adapters/read model, verified and unresolved cases, partial outputs, route and responsive UI, and complete staged browser QA, managed-runtime verification, combined regression, independent review, and capability freeze. No commit/push/deploy/.env/secrets/paid API/external writes/destructive actions.

workstreams.name: Canonical Lead Workspace read model and endpoint
workstreams.operatorOutcome: A single read-only, versioned workspace payload composes existing records without recalculating WS1-WS3 conclusions.
workstreams.requiredDataState: existing verified and unresolved records; partial legacy data tolerated

steps: Open workspace API for an existing record and verify canonical states are represented honestly.

failureConditions: provider payload leaks into UI contract

requirements.text: One versioned Lead Workspace read model composes existing records and canonical shared services.
Department outputs preserve provenance, freshness, confidence, completeness, blockers, dependencies, and recommended actions.
WS1 acreage, WS2 evidence/research, and WS3 readiness/five strategies are consumed, not recomputed.

repairHistory.summary: Read model now composes the canonical WS1 acreage basis from operatorRecord.identity.acreageBasis; legacy report.reconciliation is a fallback only when no canonical basis exists (system-wide in src/landos/lead-workspace.ts, not per-property)
repairHistory.regressionCoverage: src/landos/lead-workspace.test.ts: 'renders the canonical identity acreage basis, never the legacy reconciliation, when both exist' and 'tolerates partial legacy data'

repairHistory.summary: Deal Library rows now route to the Lead Workspace via a new optional onOpenDeal prop on DealCard; Acquisitions passes openDeal which also records the ?deal= deep link for refresh persistence; legacy in-place card open preserved for legacy LandOS spine
repairHistory.regressionCoverage: journey lead-workspace-acquisitions-readonly new steps: library click path lands on lead-workspace-root exactly once and deal-card-root stays absent (permanent browser assertion)

repairHistory.summary: LeadWorkspace.tsx double-encoded UTF-8 fixed; header middot and loading ellipsis now ASCII-safe escapes
repairHistory.regressionCoverage: journey lead-workspace-acquisitions-readonly new forbid_text mojibake step (permanent browser assertion against double-encoded UTF-8)

repairHistory.summary: Read model consumes the recorded resolution snapshot: resolutionState reflects the recorded state after an attempt, property.resolution preserves attempted/confidence/basis/provenance and identityConflict; route passes readResolutionSnapshot(id)
repairHistory.regressionCoverage: src/landos/lead-workspace.test.ts: 'surfaces recorded resolution provenance instead of the stale report label', 'reports not_run honestly', 'discloses a recorded APN identity conflict as a blocker'

workstreams.name: Responsive Acquisitions Lead Workspace
workstreams.operatorOutcome: Tyler opens a lead from Acquisitions and gets one coherent progressive-disclosure workspace.
workstreams.requiredDataState: real existing records; no production seed data

steps: Open an existing lead from pipeline
inspect canonical summary
open deeper evidence/activity
refresh and return
repeat at mobile width

failureConditions: legacy Deal Card tab aggregation feeds new UI

requirements.text: The live workspace presents identity, seller, canonical acreage, research, market/value, five strategies, evidence, activity, blockers and next action.
The Acquisitions path uses the new workspace while existing legacy deep links remain compatible.
Desktop and narrow mobile views are usable with honest unknown, stale, unresolved and blocked distinctions.

repairHistory.summary: Official public parcel lane now joins the wrong-parcel comparison: every official-lane resolved APN becomes a conflict candidate, and a requested-APN/requested-address pair resolving to two DIFFERENT official parcels is a hard identity contradiction (neither parcel verifies). Fixed in src/landos/property-resolution-engine.ts Lane 3 + decision gate, system-wide for every county adapter. Deal 23 QA fixture corrected to the honest conflicted state (activity-logged, no deletes)
repairHistory.regressionCoverage: property-resolution-engine.test.ts: nonexistent-APN address-match hard stop, requested-APN-vs-requested-address two-parcel hard stop naming both parcels, agreeing-address control

repairHistory.summary: Accepted-identity preservation enforced at both storage boundaries: upsertPropertyCard returns an implicitly-matched verified card unchanged and persistParcelIdentityFromResolution never replaces a confirmed verdict without explicit operator confirmation. Deal 19 restored from the QA-captured pre-intake payload and activity-logged for Tyler review.
repairHistory.regressionCoverage: property-card.test.ts accepted-identity preservation suite; parcel-identity.test.ts accepted-identity preservation suite

repairHistory.summary: Read model marks a pre-verification snapshot historical and carries the current verified provenance; the workspace Resolution box renders verified provenance first, labels the old attempt as historical and superseded, and suppresses the contradictory confidence chip. Verified leads without snapshots state their verification instead of claiming no attempt was recorded.
topComps caps per lane so validated actives always render beside the sold value basis, and compsShowingLine states exactly how many of the validated records are visible.

phases.detail: Operator-facing LeadWorkspace.tsx rebuilt: identity header + resolution chip (verified/candidate/unresolved/conflict/not-run), WRONG PARCEL hard-stop banner, blockers/Tyler decisions, identity+resolution provenance, canonical acreage basis table with dispute callout, research/evidence status, unified readiness grid, market/valuation/comps with normalized price-per-acre and honest unavailable states, exactly five approved strategies with pricing gate, seller, evidence/documents/visuals, work, activity; pure helpers in web/src/lib/lead-workspace-view.ts
lead-workspace-view.test.ts 9 tests + lead-workspace.test.ts 6 tests + acquisitions-workspace.test.ts 9 tests pass; vitest include extended to web/src so web lib tests actually run in the suite
Full suite 266 files / 3410 tests pass including routes integration and system consistency regressions after the canonical strategy rename
vite build && tsc succeeded; managed runtime restarted on the fresh bundle
npm run landos:status: exactly one verified managed server PID 159056, port 3141, root+health 200, serving the fresh WS2 production bundle

steps: Open http://localhost:3141/dept/acquisitions?deal=19 in the dashboard (authenticated).
Expand the 'Canonical acreage, research & readiness' disclosure.
Read the 'Acreage basis' block and compare with 'Identity & resolution' and the readiness/pricing blockers on the same page.
findings.expected: The Acreage basis block presents the canonical WS1 acreage basis: assessed 1.32 ac vs mapped 1.15 ac, disputed=true, Tyler decision required, sources 'Pickens County assessor roll' / 'Pickens County GIS geometry' (official) — consistent with the acreage-conflict blockers shown elsewhere on the page.
findings.actual: The Acreage basis block renders the legacy field reconciliation instead: primary '1.15 ac', primarySource 'Provider verification', primaryTier 'provider', conflict=false, status 'reconciled' — directly contradicting the same page's identity record (acreageConflict=true) and blockers ('Acreage is conflicted (assessed vs mapped)'). Root cause verified in source: buildLeadWorkspace reads input.operatorRecord.acreageBasis (does not exist at top level; canonical basis is at operatorRecord.identity.acreageBasis) and silently falls back to report.reconciliation (src/landos/lead-workspace.ts line 48). Deal 22 shows the same wrong 'Provider verification' source for an official SC statewide parcel layer basis.
findings.apiOrDbEvidence: GET /api/landos/lead-workspace/19: property.canonicalAcreage.acreage = {primary:'1.15 ac', primarySource:'Provider verification', primaryTier:'provider', conflict:false, status:'reconciled'} while property.identity.acreageBasis = {entries: assessed 1.32 (Pickens County assessor roll, disputed:true), gis_geometry 1.15 (disputed:true), tylerDecisionRequired} and strategies.pricingBlockers includes 'Acreage is conflicted (assessed vs mapped)…'. Saved payload: agent-ws1-20260716T04/deal19-verified-api.json.
findings.suspectedSubsystem: lead-workspace read model composition (src/landos/lead-workspace.ts acreage source selection)

history.detail: Read model now composes the canonical WS1 acreage basis from operatorRecord.identity.acreageBasis; legacy report.reconciliation is a fallback only when no canonical basis exists (system-wide in src/landos/lead-workspace.ts, not per-property)

steps: Open http://localhost:3141/dept/acquisitions?section=library.
Click the deal row '200 Sid Edens Rd, Pickens, SC, 29671' (#19).
findings.expected: The Lead Workspace opens (same surface as /dept/acquisitions?deal=19); no silent fallback to the quarantined legacy Deal Card.
findings.actual: The full legacy Deal Card opens (data-testid deal-card-root, 'Deal Card model v2' badge, Overview/Property/Due Diligence/Market/Strategy/Visuals/Seller/Documents/Activity tabs, Edit/Delete buttons). The same deal shows two different surfaces depending on entry path; the legacy card states the acreage CONFLICT that the workspace's Acreage basis block denies (finding F1), so the two paths contradict each other. Cause: web/src/pages/Acquisitions.tsx line 90 renders the legacy DealCard component for the library list and its internal row-click handler opens the legacy detail card instead of routing through openDeal/LeadWorkspace.
findings.suspectedSubsystem: Acquisitions library routing (web/src/pages/Acquisitions.tsx / legacy DealCard quarantine)

history.detail: Deal Library rows now route to the Lead Workspace via a new optional onOpenDeal prop on DealCard; Acquisitions passes openDeal which also records the ?deal= deep link for refresh persistence; legacy in-place card open preserved for legacy LandOS spine

steps: Open any Lead Workspace (e.g. deal 19).
Read the small uppercase header line above the deal title.
findings.expected: Header reads 'LEAD WORKSPACE · V1.0' (clean middle dot).
findings.actual: Header reads 'LEAD WORKSPACE Â· V1.0' — a double-encoded UTF-8 middle dot (bytes C3 82 C2 B7) baked into web/src/components/LeadWorkspace.tsx (header line ~68); visible on every workspace at every width.

history.detail: LeadWorkspace.tsx double-encoded UTF-8 fixed; header middot and loading ellipsis now ASCII-safe escapes

steps: Open http://localhost:3141/dept/acquisitions?deal=20 (also reproduces on deal 21).
Read the header 'Resolution:' value and the Identity & resolution disclosure.
findings.expected: The workspace reflects the recorded resolution outcome: an attempt ran and returned state 'unresolved' with an explanatory basis (geocode-only, confidence 0.3, per landos_resolution_snapshot / landos_parcel_identity), preserving that provenance for the operator.
findings.actual: Header shows 'Resolution: Not run', implying no resolution attempt happened, and the recorded unresolved-state basis/provenance is not surfaced anywhere in the workspace. Honest in that it never claims verified, but it misstates what work occurred and drops recorded provenance.
findings.apiOrDbEvidence: landos_resolution_snapshot deal_card_id=20: state 'unresolved', confidence 0.3, basis 'Parcel not yet confirmed — only a geocoded location…'; landos_parcel_identity deal 20 state='unresolved'. API property.resolutionState='Not run'.
findings.suspectedSubsystem: lead-workspace resolutionState mapping (report.parcelVerificationStatus vs resolution snapshot)

history.detail: Read model consumes the recorded resolution snapshot: resolutionState reflects the recorded state after an attempt, property.resolution preserves attempted/confidence/basis/provenance and identityConflict; route passes readResolutionSnapshot(id)

steps: Open Acquisitions - New Lead.
Type: QA FIXTURE APN CONFLICT (independent browser QA, additive test lead, not a real seller): 222 MCDANIEL AVE, PICKENS, SC 29671 APN 4180-07-78-9999
Click Run Property Intelligence (the address true APN is 4180-07-78-1710; the supplied APN does not exist).
Open the resulting Lead Workspace (deal 23).
findings.expected: identityConflict recorded (requested 4180-07-78-9999 vs resolved 4180-07-78-1710); WRONG PARCEL - HARD STOP banner (data-testid lead-workspace-conflict) naming both APNs; chip BLOCKED - WRONG PARCEL; not verified; no property intelligence, valuation, offer range, actionable strategies, or seller brief. The engine unit test (property-resolution-engine.test.ts:88) expects this for requested-vs-resolved APN mismatch.
findings.actual: acquire/run returned matched:true parcelVerified:true confidence:1 and created deal 23. Workspace shows Verified parcel with the fabricated APN 4180-07-78-9999, the real parcel facts attached (PICKENS COUNTY OF, 47.45 ac, appraised $569,400), identityConflict null, pricingAllowed true, $1,396,881 (high confidence) valuation, Cash Flip viable. Internal contradictions render: blockers say Valuation not ready beside the $1.39M value; Resolution box says Verified parcel - No resolution attempt has been recorded for this lead. Persists across refresh and managed restart. The other conflict shape (real address + APN of a different existing parcel) also produced no conflict (see dedupe finding). The hard-stop banner is therefore unverifiable live.
findings.apiOrDbEvidence: GET /api/landos/lead-workspace/23: property.identity.apn=4180-07-78-9999, resolutionState Parcel verified (SCDOT GIS mirror), resolution.identityConflict=null, strategies.pricingAllowed=true, market.valuation.primary $1,396,881; landos_parcel_identity deal 23 state=confirmed confidence=1; logs/main.log acquire_run matched:true parcelVerified:true dealCardId:23.
findings.suspectedSubsystem: property-resolution-engine conflict detection / county-GIS lane APN handling

history.detail: Official public parcel lane now joins the wrong-parcel comparison: every official-lane resolved APN becomes a conflict candidate, and a requested-APN/requested-address pair resolving to two DIFFERENT official parcels is a hard identity contradiction (neither parcel verifies). Fixed in src/landos/property-resolution-engine.ts Lane 3 + decision gate, system-wide for every county adapter. Deal 23 QA fixture corrected to the honest conflicted state (activity-logged, no deletes)

steps: Open Acquisitions - New Lead.
Type: QA FIXTURE APN CONFLICT (independent browser QA, additive test lead, not a real seller): 222 MCDANIEL AVE, PICKENS, SC 29671 APN 5105-00-44-0497
Click Run Property Intelligence (that APN belongs to 200 Sid Edens Rd = existing deal 19; the address is the Pickens County courthouse).
Observe it opens existing deal 19 as verified; compare deal 19 identity records before/after.
findings.expected: Hard stop for the address/APN contradiction; at minimum no mutation of existing deal 19 records without Tyler confirmation.
findings.actual: No new record and no conflict: the engine matched deal 19 by APN, re-ran resolution and browser vision against it (log browser_vision cardId:19 merged:5), returned matched:true parcelVerified:true confidence:1, and OVERWROTE accepted records: owner ELROD MELINDA KAY became ELROD MELINDA K (ownerRaw too, no ownerWarnings); parcel-identity basis changed from the LandPortal Map Search browser verification (confidence 0.9, with second-lane caveat recorded) to Parcel identity verified by South Carolina statewide parcel layer (SCDOT GIS mirror) - Pickens County (confidence 1.0); resolutionState provenance now cites SCDOT instead of the original LandPortal browser verification; county label Pickens County became Pickens. Accepted operator information changed without Tyler confirmation.
findings.apiOrDbEvidence: Pre-intake payload owner ELROD MELINDA KAY / county Pickens County vs post-intake ELROD MELINDA K / Pickens; landos_parcel_identity deal 19 updated_at 1784178681, confidence 0.9 to 1, basis rewritten; logs/main.log acquire_input/acquire_lanes/browser_vision/acquire_run for the QA FIXTURE APN CONFLICT input resolving to dealCardId 19.
findings.suspectedSubsystem: acquire dedupe/merge path (existing-deal APN match re-runs resolution and persists over accepted identity records)

history.detail: Accepted-identity preservation enforced at both storage boundaries: upsertPropertyCard returns an implicitly-matched verified card unchanged and persistParcelIdentityFromResolution never replaces a confirmed verdict without explicit operator confirmation. Deal 19 restored from the QA-captured pre-intake payload and activity-logged for Tyler review.

steps: Open http://localhost:3141/dept/acquisitions?deal=19.
Expand Identity & resolution and read the Resolution box.
findings.expected: A verified lead Resolution box presents the verification provenance, or clearly separates the historical geocode-era snapshot from the current verified state.
findings.actual: One box renders chip Verified parcel + confidence 70% + basis Parcel not yet confirmed - only a geocoded location and/or operator-supplied identifiers support it + Credible evidence from 1 source(s) resolves the intended property (confidence 0.70) - three mutually contradictory statements in one panel (pre-existing before this run intake). Deal 23 variant: Verified parcel + No resolution attempt has been recorded for this lead.
findings.apiOrDbEvidence: GET /api/landos/lead-workspace/19: property.resolutionState Parcel verified (...) while property.resolution carries the stale candidate snapshot (attempted:true, confidence:0.7, basis Parcel not yet confirmed...) never superseded after verification.
findings.suspectedSubsystem: lead-workspace read model property.resolution snapshot passthrough + Resolution box rendering

history.detail: Read model marks a pre-verification snapshot historical and carries the current verified provenance; the workspace Resolution box renders verified provenance first, labels the old attempt as historical and superseded, and suppresses the contradictory confidence chip. Verified leads without snapshots state their verification instead of claiming no attempt was recorded.

steps: Open deal 19 and expand Market, valuation & comparables.
Count comp rows and look for any validated ACTIVE listing row or a way to see more than 8 rows.
findings.expected: Rendered rows show normalized $/acre (they do) and the validated active listings advertised by the counts line (51 validated active) are reachable in the UI.
findings.actual: topComps() concatenates sold-then-active and slices to 8, so with 55 validated sold no active row can ever render and there is no control to see more; 98 of 106 validated unique registry rows (including the active comp at $186,792/ac, 245 and 301 Railroad St) exist in the payload but are invisible everywhere in the workspace.
findings.apiOrDbEvidence: Payload market.comparables counts validatedSold:55 validatedActive:51 uniqueProperties:106; rendered table shows 8 sold rows only; web/src/lib/lead-workspace-view.ts topComps() slice logic.
findings.suspectedSubsystem: lead-workspace-view topComps row selection / comps table UX

history.detail: topComps caps per lane so validated actives always render beside the sold value basis, and compsShowingLine states exactly how many of the validated records are visible.

Recheck payload: property.canonicalAcreage deep-equals property.identity.acreageBasis on deal 19 (disputed basis, Tyler decision) and deal 22 (county sources); property.resolution carries attempted/state/confidence/basis on deals 20/21

vitest: lead-workspace.test.ts 6 tests (canonical-basis precedence, legacy fallback, resolution provenance, honest not_run, APN-conflict blocker) + operator-qa-runner.test.ts 16 tests (incl. apn_conflict genuine-conflict selection) all pass

Refresh persistence verified: deep link and library-click path both restore the Lead Workspace after browser reload (independent recheck + automated journey qa-2026-07-16T04-44-34-620Z)

vitest full suite: 266 files, 3410 tests, 0 failures (2026-07-16T01:04 local) incl. new web/src/lib/lead-workspace-view.test.ts (9 tests: money/ppa/acres honesty, resolution chip precedence incl. conflict hard-stop, comp rows with honest null ppa, readiness rows, acreage entries, strategy rows) and updated acquisitions-workspace source-shape tests

Workspace UI renders only the versioned /api/landos/lead-workspace/:id payload via pure view-model helpers; no WS1-WS3 recomputation client-side; canonical five strategies renamed system-wide per Tyler's prompt (Cash Flip, Novation or Double Close, Subdivide or Minor Split, Land-Home Package, Improvement Then Flip) in strategy-readiness.ts and all consumers
Managed runtime serving the fresh WS2 bundle; root and health 200

Round-2 repairs applied and verified via live payloads: deal 19 fully restored, deal 23 honest conflicted state, conflicted intakes get their own record, gated screening runs contribute no facts. Details in qa-report-ws2-independent.md Recheck 2 section.

Full suite 3422 tests pass including new regressions: engine wrong-parcel hard stops, accepted-identity guards, gated screening run contributes nothing, historical resolution provenance, per-lane comp visibility.

Independent recheck 3: deal 19 fully restored and byte-identical through a repeat conflict-shaped intake; deal 23 and 24 honest conflicted with no fabricated facts; conflict route regression proven live; sweep clean. No open findings remain across the WS2 report and rechecks.
Recheck screenshots: deal 24 hard-stop banner desktop and legacy, deal 23 corrected desktop, deal 19 restored desktop and after managed restart, mobile 412x915 captures
Refresh persistence verified on deals 19, 20, 21, 22, 23, 24 across the WS2 QA runs including the library click path with the ?deal= deep link
Managed-restart persistence verified: deals 19 and 24 reopened identical after npm run landos:restart during rechecks 2 and 3, and deal 23 during recheck 2

Automated journeys on the final bundle: lead-workspace-acquisitions-readonly PASS and genuine-apn-conflict PASS, both real browser, incl. library click path, mojibake guard, five strategies, both viewports

Combined operator regression qa-2026-07-16T12-36-55-717Z: 11 pass including every Lead Workspace journey and the fixed genuine-apn-conflict; 1 fail strong-comp-market from the pre-existing comp-registry-unit-suffix-under-merge defect owned by paused sprint-2026-07-15-dealcard-v2 ws4-comps-valuation per Tyler direction; thin-comp-market honestly fixture-unavailable, also WS4-owned; both mutation journeys correctly refused

finalRegression.detail: All journeys in this sprint's scope pass in a real browser; the combined picture improved from 10 to 11 passing journeys with genuine-apn-conflict fixed system-wide this sprint. The single remaining failure, strong-comp-market, is the pre-existing legacy Deal Card defect comp-registry-unit-suffix-under-merge formally owned by the paused dealcard-v2 sprint workstream ws4-comps-valuation, deferred there by Tyler's explicit direction; thin-comp-market remains honestly fixture-unavailable and is excluded from this sprint's acceptance matrix, its fixture decision owned by WS4.

finalReview.detail: landos-final-reviewer: Independent reviewer verified ledger, QA reports, claims-lint, WS4-ownership of the single regression failure, protected capability journeys, and 49/49 live read-only browser spot-checks on deals 19/20/23/24 with API/UI reconciliation and restored accepted identity on deal 19. Sprint may complete.
